intro: Xiphopteris blepharodes, also known as Blepharostachys or Blepharostachys blepharodes, is a plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly referred to as the polypody ferns. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Xiphopteris blepharodes include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Xiphopteris blepharodes is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its health benefits.
